spec_match.rationale: The page now pairs the compatibility-mode restriction table with live core and compatibility adapter limit reads.

interactive.rationale: Core and compatibility buttons query real WebGPU adapters and populate live limit values in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: The live probe handles missing WebGPU, missing adapters, and adapter request errors with visible status messages.

references.rationale: Page cites the required chromestatus.com/feature/6436406437871616 self-heal link (CLAUDE.md rule 3).

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

openQuestions.title: Exercise more of the WebGPU Compatibility mode spec surface
openQuestions.detail: The spec (https://github.com/gpuweb/gpuweb/blob/main/proposals/compatibility-mode.md) covers more than the page demonstrates. Walk back through the spec's exposed methods, properties, or modes and add controls for the ones that materially change behaviour. Today the page only hints at a subset.

openQuestions.title: Mirror the spec's stated motivation in the demo
openQuestions.detail: ChromeStatus motivation reads: 'WebGPU is a good match for modern graphics APIs such as Vulkan, Metal and Direct3D 12. However, there are a large number of devices which do not yet support those APIs. In particular, on Chrome on Windows, 31% of Chrome users do not have Di...'. The demo should make that motivation visible — show the problem the spec is solving (before/after, with-without, attacker view) so a reader leaves understanding why the feature ships, not just what it does.

summary: Compatibility Limits now has a real live-adapter control surface; remaining follow-ups are non-major depth/motivation improvements.
